digest.top_line: PRIMARY2: PSMA-PET biopsy triage non-inferior for csPCa (12% vs 16%, diff -3.7%), avoiding biopsy in 49% of equivocal/negative-MRI men.
digest.tldr: Prostate carried the day's signal. PRIMARY2 shows PSMA-PET can triage biopsy in biopsy-naive men with non-suspicious/equivocal MRI: non-inferior csPCa detection while sparing biopsy in nearly half. Open-label, single-country ([68Ga]PSMA-11 only), and long-term miss rate of avoided biopsies still maturing.

sites.intro: Diagnostic biopsy-triage question, not a therapy signal: where PSMA-PET slots into the pre-biopsy pathway.

studies.name: PRIMARY2
studies.tldr: Non-inferior csPCa detection (12% vs 16%, diff -3.7%) with PSMA-PET biopsy triage, avoiding biopsy in 49% of equivocal/negative-MRI men.
details: 📊 Co-1° csPCa (Gleason ≥3+4, ≥10% pattern 4): 12% (39/331) PSMA-PET vs 16% (51/329) systematic biopsy
📐 Risk difference -3.7% (95% CI -8.9 to 1.5), p=0.0093; non-inferiority margin 10% met
📊 Co-1° biopsy avoided in 49% (163/331) of PSMA-PET arm (95% CI 44-55), p<0.0001 vs 20% threshold
🔍 Phase 3 non-inferiority RCT, 7 Australian sites, N=660 biopsy-naive men, 1:1, unmasked
🔍 Eligible: non-suspicious/equivocal MRI (PI-RADS 2 in 51%, 3 in 49%) + high clinical risk, PSA ≤20, ≤cT2
🔍 PET-positive (PRIMARY score 3-5) → targeted transperineal biopsy; PET-negative (1-2) avoided biopsy
🔗 Extends the earlier PRIMARY diagnostic-accuracy study into a randomised biopsy-triage design
⚠️ Open-label; no masking of participants or investigators
⚠️ Follow-up ongoing: long-term safety of avoided biopsies (missed csPCa) not yet confirmed
⚠️ Single-country (Australia), one tracer ([68Ga]PSMA-11); health-economics + other radiopharmaceuticals unvalidated

verdict.rationale: Phase 3 non-inferiority RCT met both co-primary endpoints; PSMA-PET biopsy triage diverges from biopsy-all standard. Open-label, single-country, follow-up ongoing.
verdict.audience: Biopsy-naive men, high clinical risk, PI-RADS 2-3 MRI, PSA ≤20, ≤cT2

analysis_sections.label: Design
analysis_sections.body: Multicentre non-inferiority **phase 3 RCT**, **7 Australian hospitals**, N=**660** biopsy-naive men, randomised **1:1**, unmasked, stratified by site. Recruited Mar 2022 to Aug 2025; follow-up ongoing.

analysis_sections.label: Population & inclusion
analysis_sections.body: Clinical suspicion of significant prostate cancer with non-suspicious or equivocal MRI (**PI-RADS 2 in 51%, PI-RADS 3 in 49%**) plus high clinical risk (PSA density >0.1, abnormal DRE, family history, BRCA, PSA >10). **PSA ≤20 ng/mL, ≤cT2**; median age 61, median PSA 5.2.

analysis_sections.label: Endpoints
analysis_sections.body: **Co-primary:** proportion with clinically significant cancer (Gleason ≥3+4, ≥10% pattern 4; **non-inferiority margin 10%**) and proportion of the PET arm avoiding biopsy by 6 months (**20% threshold**), by intention-to-treat.

analysis_sections.label: Results
analysis_sections.body: Both co-primary endpoints met: csPCa detection **non-inferior** with PET triage, and biopsy avoided in nearly half the PET arm. Post-biopsy AE rates similar between arms.

analysis_sections.label: Applies to
analysis_sections.body: Represents biopsy-naive men with high clinical risk but PI-RADS 2-3 MRI and PSA ≤20. Does not represent PI-RADS 4-5 MRI, prior biopsy, PSA >20, or ≥cT3 disease.

analysis_sections.label: Limitations
analysis_sections.body: Open-label with no masking; follow-up ongoing, so long-term safety of avoided biopsies is unconfirmed. Single-country, single tracer ([68Ga]PSMA-11); **health-economic and cross-radiopharmaceutical generalisability untested**.

open_questions: Safety of avoiding biopsy in PET-negative men over longer follow-up
Generalisability beyond Australia and to other PSMA radiopharmaceuticals
Health-economic impact of PSMA-PET-first diagnostic pathway

related_trials.brief_title: Image-guided Focal Dose Escalation- Primary pc Treated With Primary External Beam Hypofract.Stereotactic rt
related_trials.brief_summary: Technical advances in radiotherapy (RT) treatment planning and delivery have substantially changed RT concepts for primary prostate cancer (PCa) by (i) enabling a reduction of treatment time and by (ii) enabling a safe delivery of high RT doses. Several studies proposed a dose-response relationship for patients with primary prostate cancer (PCa) and especially in patients with high-risk features a dose escalation should lead to improved tumor control. In parallel to the improvements in RT…

related_trials.brief_title: Metastasis-directed Therapy in Castration-refractory Prostate Cancer
related_trials.brief_summary:
The aim is to define the postponement of next line systemic treatment (NEST), by the use of metastasis-directed therapy in patients with oligoprogressive castration-refractory prostate cancer. This will be defined by the NEST-free survival.

Furthermore the investigators will use 18F PSMA PET-CT as investigational imaging, to assess the predictive value and impact on treatment policy.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:

* Histologically proven initial diagnosis of adenocarcinoma of the prostate
* mCRPC setting, with testosterone level \< 50 ng/dl or 1.7 nmol/l

papers.title: Effect of [68Ga]Ga-PSMA-11 PET-CT in the diagnosis of prostate cancer in men with equivocal or clinically high-risk non-suspicious findings on multiparametric MRI (PRIMARY2): a multicentre, non-inferiority, phase 3, randomised controlled trial
